title: Why you should ask out your crush | Alex Le | TEDxYouth@Dayton
text: will you go out with me some of the most frightening words we could ever imagine saying to another individual if you like me you've had many crushes on many different people throughout your life who here today currently has a crush on someone imagine for one moment asking your crush on a date imagine you're alone with your crush you say something witty like you always do and they laugh you look at them and their hair glows in the moonlight there's a small silence and you catch them staring at you with those big brown eyes and for a couple moments the world stops and it's just you and then then you work up the courage to open your mouth and say will you go out with me your heart starts beating a little bit faster you can feel the butterflies flying around in your stomach and fear screams to you to stop you get scared thoughts raced through your mind what if I look like an idiot what it gets really awkward between us what did we start dating it and we break up or not friends anymore what if all my friends laugh at me what if climate change is real and the polar bears die and most importantly what if they don't like you back and they might not like you back but you should do it anyways and even if you were 100% sure that you are a part of the dreaded friend zone you should still ask them out why speaking from personal experience and the experience of my friends I believe that there are three main reasons we get scared to ask out the people that we like first we are scared of rejection we simply don't like getting told no second we are scared of people knowing that we got rejected when we get rejected realistically nothing really changes you weren't dating them before and you're not dating them now but the fact that you got rejected is now out there like a dark cloud looming over you you're scared you'll walk around being branded as the person who just got rejected every time you see someone talking and you think that they're looking at you then you know that they're talking about how you just got rejected or at least that's what my friends have told me because clearly nothing like this has ever happened to me before number three you're scared it will ruin your friendship so with all these fears looming

title: Why you should ask out your crush | Alex Le | TEDxYouth@Dayton
text: talking about how you just got rejected or at least that's what my friends have told me because clearly nothing like this has ever happened to me before number three you're scared it will ruin your friendship so with all these fears looming over you why would you ever take the risk and ask out your crush well for starters it can change the way you look at your life very often we make our decisions and judgments based off of the things we can get what will get me into the best college what will put the most money in my bank account what will win me that gold medal or what will land me a date with that cute guy or girl we're always focused on what we can gain but what have we flip the script around what if we began focusing on what I'm the person that we're becoming what if instead of focusing on whether or not we could get a date we begin focusing on whether or not we could stand up to our fears because at the end of the day it really doesn't matter if you can get one person to go on a date with you but it definitely matters if you are a person who can muster up the courage to stand strong in the face of to stand strong in the face of their fears and do something that you're scared of I challenge you to shift your paradigm don't view the world as a series of chances to get something you want view life as a continuous opportunity to grow into the person you want to be so if you value courage or standing up to your fears go and find things that you're scared of and do them not because it will get you a date with some cute guy or girl or because it will get you into Harvard but because doing things that you're scared of will make you a more courageous person in addition it'll also make you more confident very quickly ask yourself what makes you feel confident is it how good-looking you are is it your GPA is it how much money you have in your bank account or what car you drive very often we base our confidence off of the things we have in our current status whether or not we drive the nicest car and whether or not we are the smartest or best-looking person in the room but the things we have and our current status can change with the drop of a hat one second were the smartest funniest best-looking person in the room and then we leave our bedrooms and we go into rooms with other people and suddenly we're not so what happens to your conference then what if we began basing our confidence off of our ability to face our fears our confidence

title: Why you should ask out your crush | Alex Le | TEDxYouth@Dayton
text: rson in the room and then we leave our bedrooms and we go into rooms with other people and suddenly we're not so what happens to your conference then what if we began basing our confidence off of our ability to face our fears our confidence becomes rooted in the belief that we are a person who can stand up to our fears and that we are a person who's willing to go out of our comfort zone in order to become the person that we want to be that belief in confidence you have in yourself it's something that no one else can take away from you so when everyone so when you inevitably get rejected when everyone else feels sad and depressed you'll feel happy and confident when you look in the mirror you won't see a loser who can't get a date you'll see a person who tackles the challenges of life head-on and this will let you stand a little bit straighter speak a little bit louder and look people in the eyes when you talk to them because you'll feel more confident in who you are I remember around a year and a half ago I really like this girl and I was 90% sure that she did not like me back I remember asking my friends during lunch for advice on what to do and naturally most of them told me not to ask her out because they knew that I would probably just get rejected eventually I realized that all the reasons I should not have asked her out boiled down to fear I was scared and when I realized how scared I was of this I told myself that this is something you need to do not because you need to go on a date with this girl or because but because you need to prove to yourself that you can do something you're scared of we had planned to hang out after school for a little bit that day and talk so I knew that I had outlets so I knew that I would have an opportunity that day to ask her out the entire time after school I'm trying to work up the courage to ask her out but on the inside I'm a nervous wreck my thoughts scream at me you can do this you have to do this if you don't do this you're gonna regret it do it now do it now you scaredy-cat you can't even ask out the girl you like how are you going to do anything in life quit being a loser oh man I just missed my opportunity maybe I shouldn't do today maybe I shouldn't ask her out at all I'm just gonna get rejected right do I really even want a girlfriend we start walking out to the parking lot and despite the tornado blowing through my mind I'm cracking jokes and she's laughing and I can't help but real I

title: Why you should ask out your crush | Alex Le | TEDxYouth@Dayton
text: sk her out at all I'm just gonna get rejected right do I really even want a girlfriend we start walking out to the parking lot and despite the tornado blowing through my mind I'm cracking jokes and she's laughing and I can't help but real I can't help but smile when I realize how pretty she looks her radiant smile spreading across her face her soft laughter filling the air and her golden her golden hair shining in the sunlight in that moment she looked beautiful but the seconds were counting down and I told myself that this is it it is now or never so I took a deep breath and I counted down from five in my head five four three two one before I knew I had opened my mouth in the words had just come stumbling out will you go on a date with me I did everything I was supposed to I risked looking like a fool and I risked getting rejected and I'm proud to say that she said no she said no I got rejected but honestly at that point it really didn't matter to me anymore I was not sad or depressed because I got rejected I was ecstatic because I I knew in my head that I'd just done something and that really scared me I went home that night and flipped open my journal and wrote Alex you got rejected today but you know what great stuff man you did something you were scared of and that's more than enough and the funny thing is to this day this girl and I are still really good friends there's nothing awkward between us because I'm completely happy without everything turned out I wouldn't go back and change a single thing and finally everything in your life involves risk everything worth wanting in life involves risk and very often whether or not we get the things we want depends on our willingness to take those risks if you want to become an amazing person or do something incredible or even get slightly better at a skill of your choice it involves risk the risk of failure the risk of looking stupid and the risk of getting rejected if you ever want a girlfriend or boyfriend you have to risk getting rejected if you ever if you ever want to become a great public speaker you have to risk getting on stage and making many many mistakes and that's scary those thoughts of failure and looking stupid are scary but here's the good thing fear never changes no matter what you're scared of the feeling of fear is always the same yeah sometimes it is more extreme than others but that moment that moment you're standing face-to-face with fear and you feel smaller than a

title: Why you should ask out your crush | Alex Le | TEDxYouth@Dayton
text: ood thing fear never changes no matter what you're scared of the feeling of fear is always the same yeah sometimes it is more extreme than others but that moment that moment you're standing face-to-face with fear and you feel smaller than a mouse and you feel your entire body tense up that moment is always going to be there but it's something that you can conquer and since the feeling of fear is universal you can find opportunities to conquer it at every moment of your life that demon is always going to be there but the more you practice concrete the better you get at it in life the more you're able to muster up the courage to stand strong in the face of your fears the better off your life will be so I urge you the next time you're alone with the person you like in the cold hands of fear begin to wrap around your neck and you feel the air rush from your lungs and you feel your hands start to shake don't ask what will I get ask who will I become don't worry about looking like a fool worry about never being able to stand up to your fears so take a deep breath count down from five and stare fear dead in its cold eyes in asking triumphs will you go out with me [Applause]
